ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.quality_assessment: Good quality, text-based PDF with clear fields

invoice.quality_assessment: Good quality, text-based PDF with clear fields and structured layout

lc_check.lc_verification_reasoning: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate contains order number 17470 and invoice number 108759. Neither contains a separate LC field label or bank-term designation.

occurrences_in_invoice_files.location: Header/branding area at bottom-left of logo
occurrences_in_invoice_files.context: Company name/branding in invoice header area, along with address 'Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG'

consignor_search_all_files.conclusion: Consignor name 'Forest Papper AB' identified in invoice header/branding as 'FOREST PAPPER AB'. This constitutes an explicit occurrence per rule 4.1.0.

pairing_validation.reasoning: Criterion 1 (invoice number): Certificate references invoice 108759, but the invoice bears number 108760 — no match. Criterion 2 (consignor): 'Forest Papper AB' is identified in the invoice header/branding — MATCH. Criterion 3 (consignee): 'VIVA KAGIT VE AMBALAJ A.S.' appears in both Delivery address and Buyer fields of the invoice — MATCH. Since criteria 2 and 3 are met, pairing validation passes. The invoice number discrepancy will be evaluated in the goods description checkpoint (4.3).

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Forest Papper AB', Åsgatan 4, 41463 GÖTEBORG, SWEDEN. Invoice: 'FOREST PAPPER AB' found in header/branding area with address 'Åsgatan 4, 414 63 GÖTEBORG'. Name match after case normalization: 'forest papper ab' = 'forest papper ab'. Country: Certificate says SWEDEN. Invoice does not have an explicit 'Country' field for the issuer but VAT number SE556878121401 has SE prefix which per rule 4.1.2.1 verifies Sweden. MATCH on both name and country.
checkpoints.motivation: Consignor-namnet 'Forest Papper AB' identifieras i fakturans sidhuvud/branding som 'FOREST PAPPER AB' (regel 4.1.0). Landet Sverige verifieras via VAT-nummrets SE-prefix (regel 4.1.2.1). MATCH.

Name normalized: 'viva kagit ve ambalaj a.s.' = 'viva kagit ve ambalaj a.s.' — exact match. Minor address spelling differences ('BOLGESI' vs 'Bölgesi', 'CAD' vs 'cad.') are not relevant per 4.2.4 — only company name and country matter. Country: TURKEY = TURKEY. MATCH.

checkpoints.certificate_value: 48 Packages Fluting- Testliner, Invoice No: 108759, Order No: 17470
checkpoints.invoice_value: Description of Goods: Testliner, Invoice no: 108760, Our Order number: 17469

comparison_steps.description: No article numbers in certificate — not applicable

Certificate goods: 'Fluting- Testliner'. Invoice goods: 'Testliner'. The certificate specifies two product types: Fluting AND Testliner. The invoice only describes the goods as 'Testliner'. 'Fluting' is not found in the invoice goods description.
comparison_steps.result: Analysis: Certificate mentions 'Fluting- Testliner' which contains two product identifiers. Invoice only mentions 'Testliner'. However, examining further for Priority 3.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 - Generic goods with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Certificate references Invoice No: 108759. Invoice bears number 108760. These do NOT match. Certificate references Order No: 17470. Invoice shows Our Order number: 17469. These do NOT match either.

rules_applied.rule_description: Controlled text matching - identifying identity-bearing main designation
rules_applied.outcome: Certificate states 'Fluting- Testliner'. Invoice states 'Testliner' only. 'Fluting' not found in invoice description. Partial text match only.

rules_applied.rule_description: Generic goods with invoice reference — reference numbers must match exactly
rules_applied.outcome: MISMATCH - Certificate invoice ref 108759 ≠ invoice number 108760; Certificate order ref 17470 ≠ invoice order 17469

checkpoints.debug_explanation: The certificate describes goods as '48 Packages Fluting- Testliner' and references Invoice No: 108759 and Order No: 17470. The invoice describes goods as 'Testliner' only (no 'Fluting') and bears Invoice no: 108760 with Our Order number: 17469. Two critical mismatches: (1) Invoice number: certificate says 108759, invoice says 108760 — these differ by 1 digit and are NOT the same number. (2) Order number: certificate says 17470, invoice says 17469 — also different. Additionally, the certificate's goods description 'Fluting- Testliner' includes 'Fluting' which does not appear in the invoice. Per Priority 2 (4.3.3), the identity-bearing main designation 'Fluting- Testliner' cannot be fully identified in the invoice (only 'Testliner' is present). Per Priority 3 (4.3.5), the invoice reference does not match. Both the invoice number and order number are different. This constitutes MISMATCH. The documents appear to relate to different orders/invoices from the same parties — possibly the certificate was issued for a different shipment than the attached invoice.
checkpoints.motivation: Certifikatets fakturanummer (108759) och ordernummer (17470) överensstämmer inte med fakturans nummer (108760) respektive ordernummer (17469). Dessutom anger certifikatet 'Fluting- Testliner' medan fakturan enbart beskriver 'Testliner'. Referenserna kan inte verifieras. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice number difference (108759 vs 108760) could be a typo or OCR error
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: The difference is exactly 1 in the last digit. Both the invoice number AND the order number differ (108759 vs 108760 and 17470 vs 17469). Two separate reference mismatches strongly indicate different documents, not a typo. Per rule 4.3.5, the reference must match exactly.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The goods 'Fluting- Testliner' could be matched to 'Testliner' alone since Testliner is a type of paper product
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Per rule 4.3.3, the identity-bearing main designation must be identifiable in the invoice without semantic interpretation. 'Fluting' is a separate product type from 'Testliner' — both are paper grades but they are different products. The certificate lists both; the invoice lists only one. This is not a case of additional non-identity-bearing information.

checkpoints.invoice_value: Total Quantity 23,425 (unit: MT per line items); Line items sum: 5.004 + 7.602 + 2.877 + 7.942 = 23.425 MT

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states '46740kgs' in box 7. Numeric value: 46740. Unit: kgs (kilograms).

Looking for 46740 in invoice. Invoice Total Quantity: 23,425. This is 23425 (using comma as thousands separator in European context, or 23.425 as decimal). Line items show quantities in MT: 5.004, 7.602, 2.877, 7.942. Sum = 23.425 MT = 23425 KG. Certificate: 46740 KG. 46740 ≠ 23425. Not found.

Invoice line items are in MT. Sum: 5.004 + 7.602 + 2.877 + 7.942 = 23.425 MT. Converting: 23.425 MT × 1000 = 23425 KG. Certificate: 46740 KG. 23425 ≠ 46740. Also checking if invoice Total Quantity 23,425 could be 23.425 MT = 23425 KG → still ≠ 46740. Also checking if comma is decimal: 23.425 MT = 23425 KG ≠ 46740 KG. Also checking 46.74 MT interpretation: 46740 kgs = 46.74 MT. But invoice total is 23.425 MT, not 46.74 MT.
comparison_steps.result: MISMATCH - converted values do not match

comparison_steps.description: Sum of line items: 5.004 + 7.602 + 2.877 + 7.942 = 23.425 MT = 23425 KG. Certificate: 46740 KG. 23425 ≠ 46740. Deviation: 99.5%. Far exceeds ±0.1% tolerance.

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 46740kgs (46740 kilograms, no GW/NW specified). Invoice: Total Quantity 23,425 with line items in MT (5.004 + 7.602 + 2.877 + 7.942 = 23.425 MT). Converting via 3B: 23.425 MT × 1000 = 23,425 KG. Certificate value: 46,740 KG. The difference is 46740 - 23425 = 23315 KG. This is almost double. The certificate value (46740 KG) is approximately twice the invoice value (23425 KG). This is a massive discrepancy (approximately 99.5% deviation), far exceeding the ±0.1% tolerance. No interpretation or tolerance rule can bridge this gap. The certificate appears to reference a different shipment or invoice (as also evidenced by different invoice/order numbers). MISMATCH per rule 4.4.5.2.4 (tolerance exceeded) and main rule 4.4.1.
checkpoints.motivation: Certifikatets kvantitet 46740 kgs kan inte identifieras i fakturan. Fakturans totalkvantitet är 23,425 MT (= 23425 KG). Avvikelsen är ca 99,5 %, vilket vida överstiger tillåten tolerans på ±0,1 %. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Perhaps 46740 kgs includes packaging weight while invoice shows net weight
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: Even if there were a packaging difference, 46740 vs 23425 is nearly double. No reasonable tare/packaging weight could account for a 23315 KG difference on a 23425 KG shipment. Furthermore, the certificate does not specify GW and the invoice does not show a separate gross weight of 46740. Per rule 4.4.5.2.4, the tolerance is ±0.1% only.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The invoice Total Quantity 23,425 might use comma as decimal (23.425 MT)
rejected_hypotheses.rejection_reason: This interpretation is already considered: 23.425 MT = 23425 KG, which still does not match 46740 KG.

Invoice number: no match (108759 ≠ 108760). Consignor: Forest Papper AB found in invoice header — match. Consignee: VIVA KAGIT VE AMBALAJ A.S. found in buyer field — match. 2 of 3 criteria met. Validation passes.

rule_evaluation_log.reasoning: Invoice has Total Quantity row; already tested via 3D

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet hänvisar till fakturanummer 108759 och ordernummer 17470, men den bifogade fakturan har nummer 108760 och ordernummer 17469. Dessutom anger certifikatet en vikt på 46 740 kgs medan fakturans totalkvantitet är 23 425 kg. Dokumenten verkar avse olika leveranser och kan inte verifieras mot varandra.
overall_assessment.summary: Two critical mismatches found: (1) Goods description - certificate references invoice 108759/order 17470 while the attached invoice bears number 108760/order 17469; certificate describes 'Fluting- Testliner' while invoice describes only 'Testliner'. (2) Quantity - certificate states 46740 kgs, invoice total is 23.425 MT (23425 KG), a ~100% discrepancy. The certificate appears to have been issued for a different shipment/invoice than the one attached. Consignor, consignee, and origin match correctly.
blocking_issues: Goods description mismatch: certificate 'Fluting- Testliner' vs invoice 'Testliner'
Quantity mismatch: certificate 46740 kgs vs invoice 23425 KG (23.425 MT)
